title: salvia rosmarinus
intro: Salvia rosmarinus, also known as rosemary, is a fragrant, evergreen shrub that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its ability to improve cognitive function, boost memory, and reduce inflammation due to its high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of rosemary include its potential to improve digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and even help prevent certain diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Rosemary can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, essential oil, and capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
